issues.detail: The taxonomy test checks only recipients and channels, leaving required payload semantics uncovered: "returned-to-scoped (with reason)", "consent CTA", "fund-to-kick-off", revoked access with "replacement on dashboard", and PM auto-revert messaging that is "instructive not punitive".
issues.fix: Add observable payload assertions for each specified reason, CTA, dashboard affordance, and message-tone requirement.

issues.detail: "each recipient receives exactly ONE notification" does not define whether notification means the single logical committed event or a channel delivery. A recipient required to receive "email + in-app" necessarily observes two deliveries.
issues.fix: Separately assert one logical notification event per committed source event and one delivery per required recipient-channel pair, with no duplicate pair.

issues.detail: The requirement states only "with an anti-spam guard"; the test invents "every distinct conversation still surfaces — no silent total suppression" and does not define "distinct conversation" or a measurable bound.
issues.fix: Remove the invented surfacing guarantee. Define the guard's cap/window/coalescing behavior in the requirement before asserting exact observable counts.

issues.detail: The requirement scopes atomicity to the "Critical-event reliability guard (money, access, completion)", but AT-016.09 exercises only "a money-class event". Access issuance/revocation and completion can therefore commit without their notification event while all tests pass.
issues.fix: Parameterize the atomic fault test across every money, access, and completion transition covered by the guard.

issues.detail: The coverage map claims "Recipients resolve at creation", but the test only proves old recipient X still receives after a role change. It does not prove newly eligible recipient Y is excluded, so an implementation that re-resolves at send time and sends to both passes.
issues.fix: Change the role between creation and delivery, then assert X receives and Y does not.

issues.detail: The tests omit the ambiguous provider outcome where the provider accepts the send but its acknowledgement is lost before the sent marker is committed. This is the key collision between "an unconfirmed send retries" and "one notification per committed event" and can produce duplicates.
issues.fix: Inject a fault after provider acceptance but before the durable sent mark; verify retry behavior preserves a single logical notification and no duplicate recipient-channel delivery.
